name: Radisson Blu Hotel, London Marble Arch
detail: We stayed for 2 night and it was great staying in this hotel. The rooms were clean, the location was great and the staff was very friendly and helpful. The rooms were quite small though. But this is what you expect of London hotels. Overall a great place to stay

name: Euston Square Hotel
detail: What a surprise we got when we opened the door to our hotel room! Despite it's relatively small size (after all, all London hotel rooms are small), it felt roomy due to the well-designed recent renovation. The large bathroom with fantastic shower head was a dream. Comfy bed, plenty of power points, spotlessly clean, and minimalist design together make this the hotel to stay in whilst in London. Add to it, its proximity to Euston Square station, and a very short walk to Euston, plenty of restaurants, coffee shops and virtually anything your heart desires. We loved it so much, we are returning next week.

name: Smart Russell Square Hostel
detail: I stayed here for one night in late October. I got a bed in a shared room for very cheap, obviously this is ideal if you're a young traveler on a budget wanting to stay in central London! But saving money can come at the expense of your well being...I have a motto of 'you get what you pay for', and I think next time I'll have to spend a little more for the sake of a good nights sleep. In my opinion its false economy if you get a cheap room but the worst night sleep of your life, and thus the next day of your life is spent feeling like sh**. So yeah you saved money, but spent a lot more in terms of stress. The room I stayed in was just awful - a small, long room with very high ceilings, which meant that three triple-story bunk beds were crammed into it. The beds rocked and wobbled like crazy each time I climbed mine, and whenever the sleeper above me moved or climbed up/down, the disturbance was hideous. Someone might as well have been shaking me with their bare hands. The beds were also very small, smaller than a single bed, and cramped. No where to put luggage either. There was no ventilation except for a small fan. It was VERY hot and VERY uncomfortable. Last but not least, the noise from the neighboring room was super loud and intrusive. I know its a cheap hostel in central London, but not everyone chooses to stay in one of these because they're interested in bar crawls! All I could hear almost all night was a hoard of yelling, drunken yobs. After a night of tossing, turning, noise and a quaking bed I spent most of my morning feeling crap, and it showed. Who wants to feel like that when they're having a weekend in the city?? I don't understand why some of these cheap hostels can't have half of the hostel a more peaceful zone, and the other half a party-goers/pub-crawl zone. I don't think it would cost much to do that! The staff at reception were nice. The bathrooms were alright. Just basic, like college toilets. At least they weren't too grubby. Although I will

name: Radisson Blu Hotel, London Heathrow
detail: Unfortunately, the staff did not handle our stay way but it seemed that this was unique to us. Everyone else seemed satisfied and well taken care of. The food was the shinning star of this stay. DEFINITELY pay the extra for breakfast, it was the best we had in the UK over the course of 10 days including another Radisson Blu location. The chef is extremely accommodating since we have to be gluten free. We truly could not have asked more at the one meal we had there, breakfast. It was truly amazing! The bed was rather hard and our stay was not handled well by the staff but it did get resolved. If you are going into London, getting into London from this location is a 1.5 to 2 hour ordeal, I would NOT recommend this location if you are intending to do much in London. If your stay is on the west side or around Heathrow, then this is a good place to base from. The bus from the airport is £6 while a taxi will be around £16 but the taxi is a lot easier and you won't have to wait.

name: Cheshire Hotel
detail: This is a little gem in the heart of London. The hotel was immaculately clean throughout, the staff very friendly and helpful and the room very good. The beds were comfortable and of a high quality and good facilities in the room. The highlight of my stay was the staff. So lovely and friendly and added to the experience.

name: The Voyage Hotel Paddington
detail: Very helpfull staff,the room is very clean and you will feel the most welcome there.The location of the hotel is very strategic for sightseing most of all attractive place in London,for window shopping and very near to all public transport station such bus,tube and taxi.Highly recommended for all type of traveller.The best hotel for value your money.

name: Radisson Blu Hotel, London Euston Square (formerly Grafton)
detail: Having stayed at a few London hotels and have generally experienced some bad/discouraging service, this hotel defies this with great customer service, very inviting and warm and makes all the difference. It is located next to Warren St tube and thus very convenient to access, also within walking distance to Euston Rail (not a tube). Rooms were clean and comfortable and was given a quiet room as requested, much appreciated.

name: Strand Palace
detail: Strand Palace Hotel is the most convenient hotel in London. I have been staying there almost every couple of years for around 30 years. Rooms are very small but that is old London and you enjoy the charm of this place. Not cheap at all but the standard is matches the price. Very old fashioned, charming, old English if that’s what you like b
area: West End Soho,London - View on map

name: Smart Hyde Park Inn Hostel
detail: sharing a room with 20 others is not easy, but it's hostel life, and for $ in the center of london you can't expect luxury. the showers were hot and the beds comfortable enough. one thing you should be aware of if you bring your own laptop is that you only get 1 hour of free wifi per day.

name: Park Plaza County Hall Hotel
detail: This is an excellent hotel in London at an excellent price, the rooms are spacious and clean, it is less than a minutes walk from Waterloo Main Line station. Right next to County hall which is great for The London Eye and river trips. Just off Westminster bridge so close to parliament etc. It's a short walk along the south bank then cross the river and you are in Covent Garden.
area: Waterloo and Southwark,London - View on map
